title: pleopeltis inaequalis
intro: Pleopeltis inaequalis, also known as the unequal polypody, is a species of epiphytic fern native to Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Pleopeltis inaequalis include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an anti-inflammatory agent to reduce swelling and pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, and can also be used to make topical creams and ointments for skin conditions.
